title: Fraud Feature Sieve: Selecting Signals for a Frozen Scorer
strategies: keep every column and hand the whole wide table to the frozen scorer (reproduces the evaluator's weak all-columns baseline, ~0.1; the curse of dimensionality from noise columns caps it)
univariate |correlation| filter with a fixed generous budget (top-k): discards most pure-noise columns for a large gain but keeps spurious-correlation columns and never adapts the count per dataset

adaptive filter: rank by correlation, then pick the number of columns by cross-validating the frozen nearest-centroid model on the training log, tracking the planted informative set while dropping in-sample-spurious columns
stability / redundancy-aware selection or forward search that estimates each column's out-of-sample contribution to the frozen scorer, trading breadth of signal against added distance noise
